instruction:
There is currently a ten story dormitory with 10000 numbered rooms on each floor. There are a total of 100 people living in the dormitory, with 10 people allocated to each floor. If we do not consider the names of these 100 people and the maximum number of people per room, how many options are there?

Options:
A. $$
\left[ \frac{1 0 0 0!} {9! 9 9 9 0! 1 0} \right]^{1 0}
$$
B. $$
\left[ \frac{1 0 0 0 0!} {1 0 0! 9 9 9!} \right]^{1 0}
$$
C. $$
\left[ \frac{1 0 0 0!} {1 0! 9 9 9 0 0!} \right]^{1 1}
$$
D. $$
\left[ \frac{1 0 0 0!} {9 9 0! 1 0 0! 9} \right]^{9 9}
$$
E. $$
\left[ \frac{1 0 0 0!} {1 0! 9 9 9 0 0!} \right]^1
$$
F. $$
\left[ \frac{1 0 0! 0 0!} {1 0! 9 9 9!} \right]^{1 0}
$$
G. $$
\left[ \frac{1 0 0 0!} {1 0! 9 9 0 9 9 0!} \right]^{1 0}
$$
H. $$
\left[ \frac{1 0 0 0 0!} {1 0! 9 9 9 0!} \right]^{1 0}
$$
I. $$
\left[ \frac{1 0 0 0!} {1 0! 9 9 0! 9 9} \right]^{1 0 1}
$$
J. $$
\left[ \frac{1 0 0 0!} {1 0! 9 9 0! 1} \right]^{1 0 1}
$$
